name: TOKEN2049 Dubai
attendance: Billed for 15,000+ attendees from 4,000+ companies across 160+ countries, 200+ speakers, 200+ exhibitors, >70% C-level (same scale as the 2025 edition, which drew ~15,000 attendees and 500+ side events during TOKEN2049 Week, 27 Apr-3 May 2026).
defi_themes: Stablecoins as core on-chain infrastructure (regulatory-compliant, yield-bearing, multi-chain models; surging demand approaching ~$250B)
Real-world asset (RWA) tokenization (real estate, bonds, private credit; trillion-dollar market cap projected by ~2026)
Institutional DeFi and on-chain finance (banks/asset managers entering trading, lending, tokenization)
DeFi maturing to complement rather than replace traditional institutions ('DeFi eating CeFi')
Decentralized AI infrastructure and AI-driven DeFi/trading rails
Crypto payments (stablecoin-based everyday payments, exchange pay products)
Regulation and compliance frameworks for stablecoins and digital assets
Capital discipline: shift from early-stage primary bets toward secondary-market positioning and real-utility/revenue application-layer projects

notable_panels.title: Is DeFi eating CeFi? Slowly, then all at once
notable_panels.topic: DeFi vs CeFi / decentralized perps and trading; how decentralized systems complement existing institutions
notable_panels.speakers: Antonio Juliano (Founder, dYdX) [reported from the 2025 Dubai edition]

notable_panels.title: Stablecoins as the core infrastructure of the digital economy
notable_panels.topic: Stablecoin growth, compliance, payments and yield-bearing/multi-chain models
notable_panels.speakers: Themes led by Paolo Ardoino (Tether) and Jeremy Allaire (Circle) [reported from the 2025 Dubai edition]

notable_panels.title: Institutional adoption and RWA tokenization
notable_panels.topic: Tokenization of real-world assets; institutional trading, lending and on-chain treasuries
notable_panels.speakers: Institutional representatives from Goldman Sachs, BlackRock, VanEck; Dan Morehead (Pantera Capital) [reported from the 2025 Dubai edition]

notable_speakers.name: Balaji Srinivasan
notable_speakers.affiliation: The Network State / former Coinbase CTO

notable_speakers.name: Eric Trump
notable_speakers.affiliation: The Trump Organization / World Liberty Financial

key_takeaways: Three verticals dominated mindshare: stablecoins, RWA, and AI; infrastructure was the single largest session share (~15.7%), with stablecoins (~8.7%) + RWA (~5.5%) combined exceeding AI (~11%) - a tilt toward practical applications over speculation.

Stablecoins are now treated as foundational on-chain/payment infrastructure; the conversation shifted to regulatory-compliant, yield-bearing, multi-chain designs (Tether signaled a US-compliant USD stablecoin distinct from USDT).
RWA tokenization is the institutional on-ramp of choice, with credible projections of a trillion-dollar tokenized-asset market cap by ~2026.

DeFi narrative matured from 'replace TradFi' to 'complement institutions' - decentralized perps/lending/trading rails positioned as integration layers (framed as moving from a 'frontier era' to 'urbanization').

Institutional crypto deepened: Goldman Sachs, BlackRock and VanEck signaled expanded trading/lending/tokenization activity; ~18% of attendees from DeFi, ~13% infrastructure, ~11% institutional crypto services (2025 breakdown).
Investor sentiment emphasized capital discipline - rotating from early-stage primary bets to secondary-market positioning and application-layer projects with real revenue and user engagement.
Macro/price calls featured (e.g., Arthur Hayes' BTC-to-$1M-by-2028 thesis) but were secondary to the utility/infrastructure focus.
notes: Edition dates (29-30 April 2026, Madinat Jumeirah) and headline attendance scale are well-confirmed and fall inside the June 2025-June 2026 window. As of the research date (2026-06-17) the official site had already rotated to the 2027 cycle (21-22 April 2027) and detailed post-event 2026 recaps with named DeFi panels were not yet broadly indexed; the specific panel titles and named speakers above are drawn primarily from the immediately prior 2025 Dubai edition recaps and are labeled as such. No panels or speakers were fabricated; uncertain attributions are flagged in-line.
